description_long: This rifle features a minimalist, utilitarian design with a straight, elongated barrel and a prominent, angular magazine well. The stock is skeletonized, providing a lightweight yet sturdy rear support, and the grip is vertical and squared, likely made for ergonomic handling. The receiver is rectangular and streamlined, with a visible trigger guard and a simple trigger mechanism. The barrel is of standard length and appears to be unadorned except for a small muzzle device at the tip. The overall finish is matte, with a uniform dark gray coloration, suggesting a metal construction. There are no visible advanced optics or attachments, only basic iron sights integrated into the top of the receiver.
description: A matte dark gray, skeletonized rifle with a straight barrel, angular magazine, and basic iron sights. The design is utilitarian and lightweight, with a minimalist stock and grip.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile, including the skeletonized stock, straight barrel, vertical grip, and magazine well.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular barrel opening, squared magazine, and the narrow profile of the stock.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the first but with the ejection port and trigger guard more visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the open structure of the skeletonized stock and the alignment of the barrel and receiver.
